config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama karena tanpa perlindungan hak pribadi, kepentingan masyarakat bisa dengan mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai lewat aturan yang adil, bukan dengan mengorbankan martabat dan kebebasan orang per orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari individu yang haknya dihormati.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically come before the individual, because in the United States we have seen how "the common good" can be used to excuse abuse, discrimination, and government overreach. That said, individual rights are not absolute in every situation; public safety, fraud prevention, and basic order sometimes require limits. My view is that the burden should be on society to justify any restriction on personal rights, not on the individual to prove they deserve them. A healthy society is one that protects people first and only narrows rights when there is a clear, narrow, and necessary reason.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman saya melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan suara minoritas, membatasi kebebasan, atau membenarkan kebijakan yang tidak adil. Hak individu memang bisa dibatasi, tetapi hanya kalau ada alasan yang sangat jelas, proporsional, dan benar-benar perlu, bukan sekadar karena mayoritas merasa paling benar. Masyarakat yang kuat justru terbentuk ketika negara dan warga sama-sama menghormati martabat tiap orang, bukan ketika individu diminta selalu mengalah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the individual as a general rule, because in the United States that kind of language has too often been used to justify censorship, discrimination, and unnecessary state power. The hard truth is that “the common good” sounds noble, but it can become a blank check unless there are strict limits and real accountability. I do think some rights can be limited for public safety or basic order, but only in narrow cases where the harm is clear and the restriction is proportional. My starting point is that individual rights are the default, and society has to earn any exception.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Di Indonesia, saya melihat alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan kelompok kecil, membungkam kritik, atau melegitimasi kebijakan yang tidak adil, jadi saya tidak percaya pada kalimat itu kalau dijadikan pembenaran yang luas. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi pembatasan hak hanya sah kalau benar-benar perlu, jelas alasannya, dan proporsional. Kalau tidak, yang terjadi bukan masyarakat yang tertib, melainkan mayoritas yang diberi kuasa terlalu besar atas hidup orang lain.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general principle. From an American perspective, I am very wary of any broad claim about the "common good" because it has too often been used to justify censorship, discrimination, and government overreach. Social needs matter, but they should only override individual rights in narrow, clearly justified cases like public safety, and even then the restriction has to be proportional and accountable. If society can override rights too easily, then individual liberty is no longer protected, it is merely conditional.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
